name: Formosa (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the colours of the Argentine national flag and of the great rivers that frame the province
purity; the central white triangle echoing the geographic outline of Formosa
the Sun of May; the nine departments of the province represented by nine stars
the palms and forests of the Chaco and the agricultural wealth of the province

description: A celestial blue field with a large white inverted triangle reaching from the top edge nearly to the centre of the lower edge. Inside the upper part of the triangle, nine gold five-pointed stars are arranged in an arc, and along the lower edge of the field run two rows of stylised green palm-fronds.
history: Adopted by provincial law 1283 on 24 March 2000 after a public competition, the flag was designed by Carlos Alberto Wilde. Its shapes evoke the geography of Formosa — a wedge between the Pilcomayo, Paraguay and Bermejo rivers — while the nine stars mark the province's departments and the green fringe represents the palms and forests of the Gran Chaco.

features.symbolism: an inverted equilateral triangle descending from the upper edge — read as a stylised silhouette of Formosa's territory between the rivers Pilcomayo, Paraguay and Bermejo
the nine departments of Formosa Province
the palms and watercourses of the Chaco landscape
